# EchoMQ, In Depth

> The internals of the Valkey-native job system you own, canonical in Elixir: one protocol below the language line,
> and SIX pillars above it ‚Äî Overview, the Protocol, the Queue, the Bus, the Cache, and the Proof. The far side of
> every "‚Üí EchoMQ" door in /redis-patterns. Served at /echomq.

## When & why to read this

Read this to understand the bus **internals** ‚Äî not how to USE a queue (that is the pattern course, /redis-patterns)
but how one is BUILT as a protocol you own. EchoMQ's Golden Rule: the Lua scripts and the key layout ARE the
protocol, so any runtime that speaks them is a first-class peer. The wire is an owned, declared-key `emq:{q}:`
keyspace on a single **Valkey 9**, where every key of a queue carries the per-queue `{q}` hashtag so all of a queue's
keys hash to one Valkey Cluster slot and a multi-key Lua script stays legal (no `CROSSSLOT`). The voice is
**as-shipped** (no version numbers in the content); every figure is real Elixir from `echo/apps/echo_mq` (+
`echo_wire` / `echo_store`), with Lua shown as the named handle then its commented body. The visual identity is
**dark-editorial**. The worked consumer is codemojex; the architecture is /bcs.

## Navigation ‚Äî the six pillars

### Overview ‚Äî /echomq/overview  ‚úì built
One system, three pillars; the protocol below the line; the Redis-Patterns door. ‚Üê redis-patterns R0
- the-three-pillars ¬∑ the-protocol-below-the-line ¬∑ the-door

### The Protocol ‚Äî /echomq/protocol  ‚úì built  (the substrate all pillars share)
The owned keyspace (`emq:{q}:`, the `{q}` hashtag, the `{emq}:` reserve), the record/job hash, the Lua layer & EVALSHA (*scripts ARE the protocol*), immutability + the 14-char branded-id gate, the version fence (`echomq:2.4.2`). ‚Üê redis-patterns R0.2 ¬∑ R0.3 ¬∑ R2
- the-owned-keyspace ¬∑ the-record-hash ¬∑ the-lua-layer ¬∑ immutability-and-branded-ids ¬∑ workshop

### The Queue ‚Äî /echomq/queue  ‚úì built  (distribute work)
The lifecycle/state machine; jobs ¬∑ lanes ¬∑ the consumer; fair groups; batches; lifecycle controls (TTL/cancel/checkpoints); flows (orchestration). ‚Üê redis-patterns R3 ¬∑ R4 ¬∑ R6
- the-lifecycle ¬∑ jobs-lanes-consumer ¬∑ batches ¬∑ lifecycle-controls ¬∑ flows ¬∑ workshop

### The Bus ‚Äî /echomq/bus  ‚úì landing built (modules soon)  (broadcast signals + a retained log)
Pub/sub events (`EchoMQ.Events`, `emq:{q}:events`) + the retained, replayable event log (`EchoMQ.Stream` / `StreamConsumer` / `StreamRetention`, `emq:{q}:stream`), consumer groups + at-least-once, time-travel (read by mint instant), the archive (`EchoStore.StreamArchive` ‚Üí the durable floor). ‚Üê redis-patterns R5 ¬∑ R6
- (soon) the events channel ¬∑ the stream log ¬∑ consumer groups ¬∑ time-travel ¬∑ retention & the archive ¬∑ workshop

### The Cache ‚Äî /echomq/cache  ‚óã soon  (serve reads)
EchoStore ‚Äî the L1 ETS / L2 Valkey cache-aside near-cache: branded-Snowflake keyed, single-flight, jittered TTL, version-guarded, bus-coherent. ‚Üê redis-patterns R1 ¬∑ R5

### The Proof ‚Äî /echomq/proof  ‚óã soon
The black-box conformance suite, the `:telemetry`/OTel catalog, the honest benchmark vs Oban. ‚Üê redis-patterns R8

## The markdown source (deeper docs)

- **Per-page route mirrors** (the source-of-record for every page; the Bus pillar carries any `[RECONCILE]` marker here, never in the HTML): `docs/echo/echo_mq/markdown/<route>.md`
- **The program canon** (the design + the rung ladder ‚Äî the Bus pillar's deep grounding where canon runs ahead of code): `docs/echo_mq/emq.design.md`, `docs/echo_mq/emq.roadmap.md`
- **The as-built code the figures quote** (never the frozen `echo/apps/echomq`): `echo/apps/echo_mq` (+ `echo_wire`, `echo_store`); the wire identity is `echomq:2.4.2`.

## Related courses

- [/redis-patterns](/redis-patterns) ‚Äî the transferable patterns this system applies (the near side of every door)
- [/bcs](/bcs) ‚Äî the Branded Component System architecture EchoMQ realizes (manuscript chapter B3 ¬∑ the Bus)
- [/echo-persistence](/echo-persistence) ‚Äî the durable floor a trimmed / archived stream segment folds into
